__fixture__.name: managed_convenience_active
__fixture__.scenario: Managed boundary under managed_workspace identity mode. The vendor-hosted convenience layer (managed settings sync, hosted marketplace, managed AI quota, fleet admin) sits on top of the self-hosted protocols. Disabling or losing a managed service narrows the affected claim without reclassifying the underlying capability. The downgrade path names managed_service_unreachable and preserves local work under the resulting grace-degraded-managed posture.

plain_language_summary: Aureline uses the vendor-hosted managed convenience layer. Managed settings sync, hosted marketplace, managed AI quota, and fleet admin surfaces are available while the managed session is live. Local work is unaffected if the managed service is unreachable.

visible_downgrade_path.downgrade_note: If the managed service is unreachable, Aureline narrows to grace-degraded-managed. Local work continues; self-hosted IdP paths remain usable via the system browser.

preserved_local_work.note: Local work is intact. Managed features narrow only if the managed service is unreachable.
retained_capabilities: Open, edit, save, and undo local workspaces.
Search over the local workspace and navigate symbols.
Run local Git commands and commit locally.
Run local tasks and debuggers in trusted workspaces.
Use BYOK AI providers and local model execution.

reserved_rows.note: Passkey / WebAuthn step-up is supported via the system browser; embedded passkey UI is never admitted.
Reserved for the session-expiry / policy-epoch-roll / trust-downgrade reauth row.
Reserved for the managed seat-removal / org-membership-loss row; local-only work MUST remain representable.
Declares that managed deprovision MUST preserve local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI capability.
